spec.goal: Close the review-repair delivery loop so repair batches that create a new head must re-enter delivery-prepare before commit or push and then re-enter review-request for the pushed head.
success_criteria: review-repair explicitly requires delivery-prepare before any repair-batch commit or push and treats the pushed repair head as needing re-review
repo-local smoke coverage fails if that review-repair delivery handoff text regresses
home/codex/AGENTS.md states the repair-batch delivery-prepare and review-request re-entry edge for new heads
nix source smoke coverage guards the new AGENTS line
the installed ~/.codex review-repair skill copy matches the updated source skill and the live AGENTS activation can be verified
constraints: Keep the fix scoped to the review-repair to delivery-prepare to review-request lifecycle edge
Do not rename the existing skills or merge them into a new monolithic skill
Edit only the touched skill contract, the paired smokes, and the matching home/codex guidance
Use current local evidence rather than broad workflow redesign

tasks.title: Harden review-repair delivery handoff
tasks.objective: Update review-repair so any repair batch that needs commit or push must route through delivery-prepare first and then return needs_re_review for the new pushed head.
outputs: Updated review-repair procedure and hard-gate wording
Smoke assertions for the delivery-prepare and re-review handoff

tasks.title: Mirror the loop in AGENTS guidance
tasks.objective: Add explicit AGENTS wording for review-repair batches that create a new head so the lifecycle requires delivery-prepare before commit or push and review-request after the new head is pushed.
outputs: Updated home/codex AGENTS review workflow wording
Source smoke assertions for the new loop-closure line

tasks.title: Verify and sync runtime surfaces
tasks.objective: Run the focused verification set, sync the installed review-repair skill copy, and verify the live AGENTS surface after activation or equivalent source-backed update.
outputs: Focused verification evidence for the closed loop
Verified live AGENTS surface for the new rule

triggers: Closing the loop requires renaming skills instead of wording-only contract changes
The AGENTS update would need broader orchestration semantics beyond the review-repair edge
Verification shows the installed runtime cannot be updated through the expected local sync path

evidence: 2026-03-19: Local probe found that home/codex/AGENTS.md already requires delivery-prepare before any git commit or push, but review-repair itself does not yet state the repair-batch re-entry through delivery-prepare.

2026-03-19: Updated review-repair/SKILL.md and dev/review-repair/run_smoke.py so repair batches that need commit or push must re-enter delivery-prepare and return needs_re_review for the new pushed head; verified with python3 dev/review-repair/run_smoke.py.

2026-03-19: Updated /Users/xavier/Documents/nix/home/codex/AGENTS.md and dev/smoke/codex/run_scout_skeptic_source_smoke.py to state and guard the same review-repair to delivery-prepare to review-request loop; verified with python3 dev/smoke/codex/run_scout_skeptic_source_smoke.py.

2026-03-19: Synced /Users/xavier/.codex/skills/review-repair/SKILL.md to the new source contract, ran sudo -n -H darwin-rebuild switch --flake /Users/xavier/Documents/nix#acg-box, and verified the live /Users/xavier/.codex/AGENTS.md surface now contains the new review-repair loop-closure line.

2026-03-19: Self-review found that dev/review-repair/run_smoke.py only matched loose substrings for the new handoff; tightened the smoke to assert the exact delivery-prepare and re-enter-review-request contract sentences and re-verified with python3 dev/review-repair/run_smoke.py and git diff --check.

local_checkpoint.current_theory: The CI regressions after review repair come from a missing explicit contract edge between review-repair and delivery-prepare rather than from naming or lifecycle-series structure.
local_checkpoint.contradictory_evidence: If review-repair already had explicit commit or push handoff wording and smoke coverage, the root cause would instead be orchestration outside the checked-in contracts.
local_checkpoint.missing_evidence: Need to patch the skill and AGENTS wording, then verify the focused smokes and runtime sync surfaces.
local_checkpoint.next_action: Update review-repair and its smoke first, then mirror the same loop closure in home/codex/AGENTS.md and verify both source and runtime copies.
